search_queries_used: site:link.springer.com 10.1186/s41038-019-0175-y keloid clinical diagnosis scar boundary symptoms
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic JSS classification and site-specific treatment protocols
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Commercial or promotional material was not accepted as evidence.
The workflow cannot calculate a complete JSS score and no treatment, procedure, dose, radiotherapy, laser, surgery, referral, or interval was mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with a clinician-suspected or established keloid after injury, surgery, burn, inflammation, or another wound; pediatric, pregnancy, acne, burn, and contracture contexts need additional evidence.
setting_applicability: UAE primary care, dermatology, plastic surgery, burn, and specialist scar follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International scar consensus is relevant to a diverse UAE population, but local skin-pigmentation context, specialist pathways, consent, procedure availability, and referral policy remain required.
recency_verification: The primary publisher full text published in 2019 was reviewed on 2026-07-14; material recency uncertainty remains.
superseded_check: No newer Japan Scar Workshop consensus replacing the 2018 document was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact scar diagnostic section supports documenting a possible or established keloid, including onset, progression, location and growth beyond the original wound boundary, while explicitly preserving uncertainty between keloid, hypertrophic scar, mature scar, and mimics.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing mature scars, hypertrophic scars, keloids, and mimics; documenting scar growth within or beyond the original wound, shape, elevation, redness, hardness, progression, risk context, visual inspection, palpation, and diagnostic uncertainty.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The specialist assessment points support documenting clinician-assessed severity, subjective symptoms, functional impact, lesion site and prior response; no JSS score, procedure, treatment, or referral is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording a clinician-established scar type, subjective symptoms, lesion location, severity context, prior response, movement or tension context, and completed specialist assessment without mapping any treatment instruction.

unresolved_source_gaps: Original wound cause and date, dimensions, colour, thickness, firmness, itch, pain, ulceration, discharge, inflammation, functional restriction, family and prior-keloid history, wound complications, prior treatment, photography, investigations, diagnosis, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
